metadata.name: Audio & Deepfake Voice Dataset
metadata.description: Reference data for AI-generated voice (deepfake/vishing) detection. Includes dataset provenance, linguistic patterns derived from research, and training scenarios. Used to enhance the vishing detection engine.

sources.name: ASVspoof 2019 – Logical Access (LA) Partition
sources.description: Benchmark dataset for automatic speaker verification spoofing and countermeasures. Contains 121,461 utterances. LA partition covers Text-to-Speech (TTS) and Voice Conversion (VC) attacks using 17 spoofing algorithms including WaveNet, Tacotron, and Griffin-Lim vocoder. Widely used to train deepfake voice detectors.
sources.citation: Nautsch, A. et al. (2021). ASVspoof 2019: spoofing countermeasures for the detection of synthesized, converted and replayed speech. IEEE TASLP.

sources.name: ASVspoof 2021
sources.description: Extended version covering phone-channel and codec conditions realistic for telephone-based vishing attacks. Includes codec degradation (GSM, G.711, G.722) that mimic real phone call conditions.
sources.citation: Liu, X. et al. (2023). ASVspoof 2021: Towards Spoofed and Deepfake Speech Detection in the Wild. IEEE TASLP.

sources.name: FakeAVCeleb
sources.description: Multi-modal deepfake detection dataset with 500 real celebrity videos and 19,500 fake videos (audio+video). Audio component uses TTS, VC and lip-sync attacks.
sources.citation: Khalid, H. et al. (2021). FakeAVCeleb: A Novel Audio-Video Multimodal Deepfake Dataset. NeurIPS 2021 Workshop.

sources.name: WaveFake
sources.description: Dataset for evaluating speech synthesis detection. Contains audio from 6 neural vocoders. Used to study cross-vocoder generalisation of deepfake detectors.
sources.citation: Frank, J. & Schönherr, L. (2021). WaveFake: A Data Set to Facilitate Audio Deepfake Detection. NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks.

sources.name: ADD 2022 (Audio Deep-synthesis Detection Challenge)
sources.description: Chinese deepfake audio detection challenge dataset. Covers low-quality deepfakes, partially fake audio, and adversarial attacks on deepfake detectors.
sources.citation: Yi, J. et al. (2022). ADD: Audio Deep Synthesis Detection. ICASSP 2022.

detection_models.description: State-of-the-art models for AI-generated voice detection. These inform the feature patterns used in Phish X vishing analysis.

models.name: LCNN (Light CNN)
models.description: Baseline model used in ASVspoof challenges. Analyses spectral features absent in natural speech.

models.name: RawNet2
models.architecture: End-to-end deep neural network on raw waveform
models.description: Processes raw audio directly. Captures phase information lost in spectral transforms.

models.name: Wav2Vec 2.0 + Classifier
models.description: Fine-tuned Wav2Vec 2.0 for deepfake detection. Leverages large pre-trained speech representations.

models.name: AASIST (Audio Anti-Spoofing using Integrated Spectro-Temporal graph attention networks)
models.description: State-of-the-art model that jointly models spectral and temporal artefacts in synthesised speech.
models.citation: Jung, J. et al. (2022). AASIST: Audio Anti-Spoofing using Integrated Spectro-Temporal Graph Attention Networks. ICASSP 2022.

models.name: Phish X Vishing Detector (Heuristic)
models.architecture: Regex pattern matching on transcribed text
models.features: Linguistic markers from ASVspoof + vishing research
models.description: Phish X's built-in vishing detector analyses transcribed speech text for 50+ suspicious patterns derived from vishing research.

linguistic_deepfake_indicators.description: Linguistic and prosodic features that distinguish AI-generated/deepfake speech in vishing contexts. Used to train pattern matchers.
over_formal_phrases: I hope this call finds you well
scripted_patterns: press 1 to speak to an agent
your call is very important to us
vishing_request_patterns: can you confirm your date of birth
what is your mother's maiden name
can you read out the numbers on your card
we need your National Insurance number
please provide your full card number
can you confirm the last 4 digits
we need to verify your PIN
can you read out the OTP
urgency_language: this will be passed to debt collectors

ai_voice_acoustic_artefacts.description: Acoustic characteristics of AI-generated speech detectable by spectral analysis tools.
artefacts: Absence of natural breath sounds between phrases
Lack of filled pauses (um, uh, er)
Periodic artefacts in high-frequency bands (WaveNet, Tacotron)
Spectral discontinuities at splice points (VC attacks)

training_scenarios.title: Bank automated fraud alert (TTS)
training_scenarios.transcript: Hello. This is an automated message from your bank's fraud prevention team. We have detected unusual activity on your account. To prevent your account being suspended, please press 1 now to speak with a security advisor.

training_scenarios.title: HMRC tax debt threat (VC attack)
training_scenarios.transcript: Good afternoon. I am calling from HMRC, the tax office. Our records show you have an outstanding tax debt of one thousand four hundred and ninety two pounds. If this is not paid today, a warrant will be issued and bailiffs will attend your property.
ai_indicators: HMRC does not call with bailiff threats

training_scenarios.title: IT support password reset (TTS)
training_scenarios.transcript: Hi there. This is a call from your IT support team. We have noticed that your corporate password is about to expire. For security purposes, we need you to verify your current password so we can process the reset. Please go ahead.
ai_indicators: IT never ask for existing password

training_scenarios.title: CEO gift card fraud (voice cloning)
training_scenarios.transcript: Hi, it's Michael here. I'm tied up in a board meeting and I can't use my phone. I need you to urgently purchase five one-hundred-pound Amazon gift cards and send me the codes by email. Please keep this confidential. I'll explain everything later.

training_scenarios.title: Bank fraud department (legitimate-style)
training_scenarios.transcript: Good morning. Could I speak with the account holder please? My name is Sarah Jenkins and I'm calling from the fraud department at Barclays Bank. We've identified a transaction on your account this morning for eight hundred and forty seven pounds at an online retailer. I just need to verify a few details with you to confirm whether this was you.
ai_indicators: vishing_request: 'verify a few details' leads to card data
training_scenarios.technique: High-fidelity voice cloning + social engineering script

training_scenarios.title: Police NCA warrant (government impersonation)
training_scenarios.transcript: This call is from the National Crime Agency. A warrant has been issued in your name for tax evasion and money laundering. You are required to attend a hearing. To avoid immediate arrest, you must call back on this number within one hour.
ai_indicators: NCA/police don't call with arrest warnings
